name: networkx
description: Comprehensive toolkit for creating, analyzing, and visualizing complex networks and graphs in Python. Use when working with network/graph data structures, analyzing relationships between entities, computing graph algorithms (shortest paths, centrality, clustering), detecting communities, generating synthetic networks, or visualizing network topologies. Applicable to social networks, biological networks, transportation systems, citation networks, and any domain involving pairwise relationships.
capabilities: Create and manipulate graph data structures including undirected, directed, and multigraph variants
Compute graph algorithms such as shortest paths, centrality measures, and community detection
Generate synthetic networks using random, scale-free, and small-world models
Read and write graphs in multiple formats including GraphML, edge lists, and JSON
Visualize networks with customizable layouts using matplotlib and interactive libraries
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown files for the NetworkX Python library. All 495 static findings are FALSE POSITIVEs. The analyzer misinterprets markdown code fences as shell execution, NetworkX function names as cryptographic algorithms, and graph operations as system reconnaissance. No executable code exists. All URLs point to official NetworkX documentation.
valueStatement: Working with graph and network data requires specialized tools for creating structures, computing algorithms, and generating visualizations. This skill provides comprehensive access to NetworkX capabilities for social network analysis, biological pathway mapping, transportation optimization, and relationship modeling.
userTitle: Analyze and visualize complex networks with NetworkX
limitations: Does not provide graph databases or persistent storage capabilities
Algorithms have O(n^2) or higher complexity for large networks; specialized libraries needed for massive graphs
Visualization limited to 2D representations; 3D networks require additional libraries

useCases.title: Social network analysis
useCases.description: Analyze relationship patterns, identify influencers, and detect communities in social media data

useCases.title: Biological pathway mapping
useCases.description: Model protein interactions, gene regulatory networks, and metabolic pathways

useCases.title: Transportation optimization
useCases.description: Optimize route planning, supply chain networks, and infrastructure design

promptTemplates.title: Basic graph creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a graph with 5 nodes and add edges between them to represent a basic network structure

promptTemplates.title: Path finding
promptTemplates.prompt: Find the shortest path between node A and node F in this graph and show the distance

promptTemplates.title: Centrality analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Compute betweenness centrality for all nodes and identify which nodes are most critical

promptTemplates.title: Network generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a Barabasi-Albert scale-free network with 100 nodes and 3 edges per new node

bestPractices: Set random seeds (seed=42) for reproducible random graph generation and force-directed layouts
Use sparse matrix formats and appropriate data structures when working with large networks
Save graphs in GraphML format to preserve node and edge attributes
antiPatterns: Using floating-point numbers as node identifiers can cause precision issues in algorithms
Loading entire large networks when only subgraph analysis is needed wastes memory
Forgetting to check graph connectivity before running algorithms that require connected graphs

faq.question: What graph types does NetworkX support?
faq.answer: NetworkX supports Graph (undirected), DiGraph (directed), MultiGraph (multiple edges undirected), and MultiDiGraph (multiple edges directed).

faq.question: How do I read a graph from a CSV file?
faq.answer: Use pandas to read the CSV, then nx.from_pandas_edgelist() with source, target, and optional edge attribute columns.

faq.question: What is the fastest way to find shortest paths?
faq.answer: Use nx.shortest_path() for single pairs. For all pairs, use nx.all_pairs_shortest_path() with an iterator to save memory.

faq.question: Can NetworkX handle directed acyclic graphs?
faq.answer: Yes, NetworkX handles DAGs with functions like nx.is_directed_acyclic_graph() and topological sorting via nx.topological_sort().

faq.question: How do I detect communities in a network?
faq.answer: Use community.greedy_modularity_communities(G) for modularity-based detection or other algorithms in networkx.algorithms.community.

faq.question: What visualization libraries work with NetworkX?
faq.answer: Matplotlib integrates natively. For interactive visualizations, use PyVis or Plotly with NetworkX graph objects.
